name: codeforge
description: A self-hosted-first coding harness extension for Visual Studio Code.

codeforge.profiles.description: OpenAI API-compatible endpoint profiles. API keys are stored separately in VS Code SecretStorage.

codeforge.activeProfile.description: Active CodeForge OpenAI API profile ID.

codeforge.model.description: Model ID to send to the active endpoint. If empty, the active profile defaultModel is used.

codeforge.model.maxOutputTokens.markdownDescription:
Maximum tokens the model may generate per turn (`max_tokens` sent to the endpoint).

- `32000` (default) — cap output at ~32k, matching Claude Code's default. Automatically bounded to half the context window (so the prompt always has room) and to the model's reported output limit, so it stays safe on small-context models.
- `0` — **No limit**: send no `max_tokens`. The endpoint/model decides; on vLLM this generates up to the remaining context window. Use this for the largest possible single response.
- `>= 1` — your own cap, bounded the same way as the default.

codeforge.agent.mode.description: Active CodeForge agent mode. Agent executes coding tasks with approved local actions; Ask answers with read-only context; Plan analyzes and proposes an implementation plan.

codeforge.network.allowlist.description: Explicit endpoint origin allowlist for OpenAI API compatible backends. Saving a custom endpoint URL adds its exact origin automatically.

codeforge.permissions.mode.description: Approval mode for local tools. Manual asks before edits and commands; Smart allows read/search but asks before edits, commands, memory writes, notebooks, or service calls; Full Auto proceeds without most prompts.

codeforge.permissions.rules.description: Permission rules. Each rule has kind, pattern, behavior, scope, and optional description.

codeforge.mcp.servers.description: Explicit MCP server configurations. CodeForge never connects to MCP servers unless configured here and requested by a tool call.

codeforge.context.maxFiles.description: Internal safety cap for automatic repo context items.
codeforge.context.maxFiles.deprecationMessage: CodeForge no longer exposes this in its settings UI. Use tool-based search/glob/read and the context window token limit.

codeforge.context.maxTokens.description: Manual context window override in tokens. Set to 0 to use the selected model context length returned by /v1/models.

codeforge.context.maxBytes.description: Legacy fallback context byte budget used when no token context length is configured or discovered.

codeforge.commands.timeoutSeconds.description: Maximum execution time for approved shell commands. This does not control model response streaming.

codeforge.requests.idleTimeoutSeconds.description: Maximum time CodeForge waits without model stream activity before stopping a model request.

codeforge.requests.streamCompletionGrace.description: Time the OpenAI adapter waits after the last streamed chunk before treating the stream as finished. Increase this for slow local models that pause between tool-call argument fragments.

codeforge.agent.maxInvalidToolCallRetrie.description: How many consecutive iterations the agent loop will tolerate when the model only produces invalid (unparseable) native tool calls before stopping with an error.

codeforge.commands.outputLimitBytes.description: Maximum stdout and stderr bytes retained per approved shell command stream.

codeforge.workers.maxConcurrent.description: Maximum number of CodeForge sub-agents (workers) that run at once. Additional spawns queue and start as running workers finish.

codeforge.memory.enabled.description: Inject CodeForge's curated memory (your agent notes and user profile) into the system prompt, and expose the memory tool so the agent can save durable facts and preferences across sessions.

codeforge.memory.charLimit.description: Character budget for the agent's MEMORY notes (environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks). The agent consolidates entries when it reaches the limit.

codeforge.memory.userCharLimit.description: Character budget for the USER profile notes (who the user is — preferences, communication style, expectations).

codeforge.memory.nudgeInterval.description: Run a background memory review every N user turns to consider saving new durable facts or preferences. 0 disables the nudge.

enumDescriptions: Curated notes only (the always-on built-in memory).
Also keep a durable, searchable fact store with holographic (compositional) recall; saved memories are mirrored into it and relevant facts are recalled into each task's context.
codeforge.memory.provider.description: Optional durable memory backend layered on top of the curated notes. Fully local; no external services.

codeforge.skills.enabled.description: Surface relevant local skills (.codeforge/skills) to the agent and let it author and refine them.

codeforge.skills.creationNudgeInterval.description: Run a background skill review every N tool iterations to build or patch a reusable skill. 0 disables the nudge.

codeforge.skills.digestBytes.description: Maximum bytes of relevant skill bodies injected into a request's context.

codeforge.review.minTurns.description: Minimum user turns in a session before the background self-improvement review can run.

codeforge.curator.enabled.description: Let the curator periodically consolidate and archive agent-created skills (.codeforge/skills). Never deletes — archives are recoverable; pinned skills are exempt.

codeforge.curator.intervalHours.description: Minimum hours between automatic curator runs (default 1 week).

codeforge.curator.minIdleHours.description: Minimum idle time before an automatic curator run, when idleness is measured.

codeforge.curator.staleAfterDays.description: Days of inactivity before an agent-created skill is marked stale.

codeforge.curator.archiveAfterDays.description: Days of inactivity before an agent-created skill is archived (recoverable).

codeforge.curator.backup.enabled.description: Take a recoverable snapshot of the skill library before each curator run.

codeforge.curator.backup.keep.description: Number of curator backups to retain.

scripts.test: npm run compile:tests && node --test out-test/test/unit/*.test.js out-test/test/integration/*.test.js
scripts.vscode:test: npm run compile && npm run compile:tests && node ./out-test/test/runTest.js
